skill.name: prompt-engineering
skill.description: A practical guide for writing clear prompts, adding context, using examples, and designing structured system prompts.
skill.summary: Create clearer prompts for AI coding, analysis, review, and structured assistant behavior.
skill.author: Doyajin174

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged Markdown code fences as Ruby backtick execution, but the file contains prompt examples, not executable shell calls. Weak cryptography and entropy alerts were also false positives from prose, headings, and Korean text; no malicious intent or prompt injection was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Reported as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The detected external command locations are fenced Markdown examples and prompt templates. They do not invoke Ruby, a shell, or any executable command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line-numbered review shows Markdown fences and Korean prompt examples only. No command interpreter, subprocess API, or executable script is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keywords
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography matches occur in descriptive text and a system prompt design heading. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain metadata and a heading about system prompt design. There is no cryptographic algorithm, key handling, or security-sensitive code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: High Entropy Markdown Content
low_findings.description: The entropy warning appears to be caused by multilingual Markdown content. The file is readable text and does not contain encoded payloads or binary data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The full file is plain Markdown with YAML front matter and Korean instructional text. No obfuscated string, decoder, or payload was found.

content.value_statement: Vague prompts often produce incomplete or inconsistent AI responses. This skill gives structured techniques for clearer requests, better context, examples, and system prompt design.
actual_capabilities: Explains clarity, context, and specificity principles for better prompts.
Shows zero-shot, few-shot, role, and stepwise prompting patterns.
Provides reusable structures for coding and system prompts.
Lists prompt anti-patterns with practical improvements.
Suggests an iterative process for testing and refining prompts.
limitations: Does not run prompt tests or measure model response quality.
Does not include provider-specific API settings or model parameters.
Does not guarantee correct answers from any AI model.
Examples are mostly focused on coding and review tasks.

use_cases.title: Write clearer coding requests
use_cases.description: Turn vague bug, API, or refactor requests into prompts with environment, context, goals, and constraints.

use_cases.title: Create assistant behavior rules
use_cases.description: Design system prompts that define identity, capabilities, limits, response format, and examples.

use_cases.title: Improve repeated AI workflows
use_cases.description: Use examples and iteration steps to make routine analysis, review, and writing tasks more consistent.

prompt_templates.title: Clarify a simple request
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite my request into a clear prompt. Ask for missing context first if needed. Request: [paste request]

prompt_templates.title: Add context and constraints
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a prompt for this task using role, context, goal, constraints, and output format. Task: [describe task]

prompt_templates.title: Build a few-shot prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a few-shot prompt with two examples and one final input. Target output style: [describe style]

prompt_templates.title: Design a system prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a system prompt with identity, capabilities, constraints, response format, and example interactions for: [assistant purpose]

output_examples.output: The improved prompt states the file, runtime, error message, expected behavior, current behavior, and allowed change scope.

output_examples.input: Create a system prompt for code review.
output_examples.output: The result defines reviewer identity, review capabilities, project constraints, and a consistent issue-focused response format.

output_examples.input: Make a prompt more reliable for repeated content review.
output_examples.output: The revised prompt includes examples, acceptance criteria, and a short iteration process for correcting weak outputs.

best_practices: State the task, context, constraints, and output format before asking for results.
Use examples when the desired pattern is hard to describe.
Refine prompts based on specific failures in the previous response.
anti_patterns: Do not ask broad questions without success criteria.
Do not include unrelated background that hides the main task.
Do not rely on role instructions alone without concrete requirements.

faq.question: Can this skill help with Claude prompts?
faq.answer: Yes. The techniques apply to Claude, Codex, Claude Code, and other instruction-following AI tools.

faq.question: Does it include ready-made prompt templates?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows structures for general tasks, coding prompts, and system prompt design.

faq.question: Is this skill only for developers?
faq.answer: No. Coding examples are included, but the principles apply to writing, analysis, review, and planning tasks.

faq.question: Does it execute code or commands?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown guide with examples and does not contain executable scripts.

faq.question: Will it make every AI answer correct?
faq.answer: No. It improves instruction quality, but model limits and missing context can still affect results.

faq.question: How should I start using it?
faq.answer: Start with a clear task, add relevant context, set constraints, and specify the output format.
